item.title: २ INTRODUCTION
item.description:
1. Introductory
The Jyotirmimaṁsā (‘Investigations on Astronomical theories’) of Nilakantha Somayaji, a prominent mediaeval astronomer of Kerala (A.D. 1444-1545), is a unique work in Hindu astronomy. Nilakaṇṭha takes astronomy primarily as a practical discipline based on experimen- tation and observation. He insists that results arrived at by computation should tally with observation and that astronomical parameters and other constants should be revised periodically towards this purpose. The present work has been composed by him to set out forcibly this point of view. Inter alia he examines critically various corrections suggested by earlier astronomers, compares the results arrived at, gives his reactions and suggests further lines of action. Quite often, this is done through simultated discussions with imaginary opponents belonging to other schools of astronomy, who propose their views (pūrva-pakṣa), while Nilakantha sets out his view (siddhanta-pakşa), after critically analysing the other views, in the manner in which philosophic dis- quisitions are couched. It is this critical approach to astronomical problems that makes the work unique in Indian scientific literature in Sanskrit.

पौलिश-रोमश-वासिष्ठ… Pañcasiddhāntikā, 1.3 7

प्रोक्ते दिने ऽपि बिम्बस्य… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 330 32

बाणवेदरसचन्द्र… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 1.32 24

भानामष्टद्विवस्वद्रि… Sūryasiddhāntaḥ, 1.37 17
भास्कराय नमस्तस्मै… Laghubhāskarīyam, 1.1 5
भूविष्कम्भार्धमथवा… — 53
भूव्यासार्धेन निहते… — 53
भौमाङ्गिरश्शनीनां… Āryabhaṭaḥ, unidentified 15
मन्दकेन्द्रगतिरर्कचन्द्रयोः… — 44
मन्दांशा दशगुणिता… Khaṇḍakhādyakam, 1.2.6 18
मन्वङ्गान्धीषु षट्चन्द्रः… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 331 34
मुनिद्वयात् भाष्यकारस्य… Mahābhāṣyaṭīkā (Pradīpa) of Kaiyaṭa on 2.4.26 3
मुहूर्ततिथिनक्षत्रं… Bṛhatsaṃhitā, 2.16 9
मृदुफलदलमादौ… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 3.36 26
यत् प्रायो श्रूयते यच्च… — 22
यदा परहितप्रोक्ता… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 333 35
यदा यश्चैव सिद्धान्ते… Parāśarahorā 4
यदि फलमवाप्तं… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 11.3 38
यश्चैव भुजावर्गः… Āryabhaṭīyam, Gaṇita. 11 50
यस्तु सम्यग् विजानाति… Bṛhatsaṃhitā, 2.26 6
युगभोगध्रुवं विद्यात्… Grahacāranibandhanam, 1.16 28
योगे ग्रहाणां… Jātakaḥ (unidentified) 4
रदद्वयं सिद्धकलोनमिन्दोः… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 3.20 26, 43
रामरन्ध्रयमप्राणं… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 331 34
राहुचन्द्रोच्चजीवानां… Grahacāranibandhanasaṃgrahaḥ, 20 11
रिपुकुलधमाप्तपितुः… — 29

लब्धरामशरघात… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 2.92 25
वरचन्द्र पिनाकिरतः… — 29

वक्रानुवक्रास्तमय… Bṛhatsaṃhitā, 1.9 9

वाक्पतेर् वसुरसाः… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 3.38 27
वाग्भावोनाच्छकाव्वात्… Mahāmārganibandhanam (?) 11, 35
विशतिस्तत्र सम्भूता… — 49
विस्तरेण तदुदितं… Sūryasiddhāntaḥ, 1.55 9
वेदरन्धरसाक्षीषु… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 331 34
देवाष्टनन्दवसु… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 1.28 23
व्योमशून्यशराद्रघग्नि… — 17
शनैः शशिकराः प्रोक्ताः… Grahacāranibandhanasaṃgrahaḥ, 22 10

शशिनि चरफलाख्यं… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 11.4 38
शिष्यस्य बुद्धिमान्द्यात्… Karaṇaratnam, 1.3 9
शुक्रशीघ्रमगणा… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 1.29 23
शून्याग्निभूशरेष्वङ्ग… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 329 32
षष्ट्यब्दानां षष्टिः… Āryabhaṭīyam, Kāla. 10 13, 19
संख्या तु तेषां… Pañcasiddhāntikā, Kartarikādhyāyaḥ 7
सदसज्ज्ञानसमुद्रात्… Āryabhaṭīyam, Gola. 49 2
सप्तज्याब्दं तु सिन्धवग्नि… — 49
सप्तदशांशर् अधिकं… Khaṇḍakhādyakam, 2.2.1 18
समद्विघातः कृतिरुच्यते… Līlāvatī, 19 51
समरेखायाः पश्चात्… Golādīpikā, 4.91 39fn.
सर्वं न्याय्यं युक्तिमत्त्वात्… Bhāgavatatataram, 11.22.25 16
सषड्भवसुदत्रात्मा… — 49
साम्प्रतम् अयनं सवितुः… Bṛhatsaṃhitā, 3.2 37
सार्धं सहस्रत्रये कल्यब्दे… Govindapaddhatiḥ 17
सिद्धान्तभेदे ऽप्ययन… Bṛhatsaṃhitā, 3.4 7
सूत्रकारा न सूत्राणि… Bhaṭṭācāryaḥ 3
सूर्यान् मृदूच्चभगणाः… Siddhāntasekharaḥ, 1.30 23
सूर्यो मरीचिमादत्ते… Taittirīyāraṇyakam, 1.2.2 22
सो ऽपि तत्त्वाब्धि… — 49
सो ऽपि षड्वसुपञ्चते… — 49
स्तम्बेरमाम्बुधिहते… Śiṣyadhīvṛddhidam, Uttara. 19 12, 20
स्थितिकाले ऽधिको ऽप्यत्र… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 330 33
स्पर्शकाले तु पदमा… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 330 33
स्पर्शोपलब्धौ पदमा… Siddhāntadīpikā, p. 330 33
स्पष्टो ब्राह्मस्तु सिद्धान्तो… Parāśarahorāvyākhyā of Govindasvāmi 4
स्फुटतिथिविक्षेपसमं… Varāhamihiraḥ 16
स्फुटयोजनकर्णेन तव… — 53
स्फुटयोजनकर्णेन लब्धं… — 53
स्मृतिः प्रत्यक्षम् ऐतिह्यं… Taittirīyāraṇyakaḥ, 1.2.1 21

item.title: ५ TECHNICAL TERMS
item.description:
APPENDIX III: GLOSSARY OF TECHNICAL TERMS

  • अंश (aṃśa): Part; numerator; degree.
  • अक्ष (akṣa): Latitude.
  • अक्षज्या (akṣajyā): Rsine latitude.
  • अक्षोन्नति (akṣonnati): Terrestrial latitude.
  • अग्र (agra): Amplitude at rising (i.e., the north-south distance of the rising point from the east-west line; or the Rsine thereof).
  • अङ्गुल (aṅgula): Linear measure, inch, digit.
  • अधिक (adhika): Additive.
  • अनुलोम (anuloma): Direct motion (opposite of vakra).
  • अयनचलन (ayanacalana): Precession of the equinoxes.
  • अवनति (avanati): Zenith distance; parallax in latitude.
  • अवम (avama) (= तिथिक्षय): Omitted lunar day.
  • अविशेष-कर्म (aviśeṣa-karma): Successive approximation, process of iteration.
  • असु (asu) (= प्राण): Unit of time equal to 4 seconds.
  • अस्तमय (astamaya): Setting (diurnal or heliacal).
  • अहर्गण (ahargaṇa) (= द्युगण): Days elapsed from epoch.
  • अहोरात्रवृत्त (ahorātravṛtta) (= द्युवृत्त): Diurnal circle.
  • आक्ष (ākṣa): Sidereal.
  • अन्त्य (antya): Last (term).
  • अन्त्यद्युज्या (antyadyujyā): Maximum day radius.
  • अपक्रम (apakrama) (= क्रान्ति): Declination, obliquity of the ecliptic.
  • अपक्रमज्या (apakramajyā) (= क्रान्तिज्या): Sine declination.
  • अपमण्डल (apamaṇḍala) (= अपक्रममण्डल): Ecliptic.
  • अयन (ayana): Northward (उत्तर) and southward (दक्षिण) motion of the Sun or other planets.
  • आर्धरात्रिक (ārdharātrika): Beginning from midnight.
  • इच्छा (icchā) (= इच्छाराशि): Requisition, being one of the three quantities in the Rule of Three.
  • इच्छाफल (icchāphala): Fruit corresponding to icchā.
  • इन्दुपात (indupāta): Ascending node of the Moon.
  • इन्दूच्च (indūcca): Higher apsis (apogee) of the Moon.
  • इष्ट (iṣṭa): Desired or given number.
  • इष्टज्या (iṣṭajyā): Rsine at the desired point on the circumference of a circle.
  • उच्च (ucca): Higher apsis, especially pertaining to the epicycle of the equation of the centre.
  • उच्चनीचवृत्त (uccanīcavṛtta): Epicycle.
  • उत्क्रमज्या (utkramajyā): Rversed sine.
  • उत्तरविषुवत् (uttaraviṣuvat): Vernal equinox.
  • उत्तरायण (uttarāyaṇa): Northward motion of the Sun.
  • कक्ष्यामण्डल (kakṣyāmaṇḍala): Mean orbit, deferent, concentric.
  • करण (karaṇa): Astronomical manual, with a contemporary epoch.
  • कर्क (karka): Sign Cancer (Kaṭaka, Karkaṭa).
  • कर्ण (karṇa): Hypotenuse.
  • कला (kalā): Minute of arc.
  • कलार्धज्याः (kalārdhajyāḥ): The 24 Rsine differences in terms of minutes.
  • कलाव्यास (kalāvyāsa): Angular diameter in minutes.
  • कलि (kali): The aeon (yuga) which commenced on Feb 18, 3102 B.C.
  • कल्यब्द (kalyabda): Number of years elapsed from the beginning of the Kali epoch.
  • कल्प (kalpa): A period of 1000 yugas, equal to 4.32 billion solar years.
  • कल्यादि (kalyādi): Commencing from the Kali epoch.
  • कल्यादि ध्रुव (kalyādi-dhruva): Zero positions of planets at the commencement of the Kali epoch.
  • काललग्न (kālalagna): Time-rising point.
  • कृति (kṛti): Square.
  • केन्द्र (kendra): Anomaly; centre of a circle.
  • कोटि (koṭi): Vertical side of a right-angled triangle; complement of bhuja.
  • कोटिज्या (koṭijyā): Rsine koṭi or Rcosine bhuja.
  • क्रमज्या (kramajyā): Sum of the sine segments taken in order.
  • क्रान्ति (krānti) (= अपक्रम): Declination.
  • क्रान्तिज्या (krāntijyā): Rsine declination.
  • क्रान्तिमण्डल (krāntimaṇḍala): Zodiacal circle.
  • क्रिय (kriya): Sign Aries (Meṣa).
  • क्षितिज (kṣitija): Horizon.
  • क्षितिज्या (kṣitijyā): Earth-sine.
  • क्षेत्र (kṣetra): Geometrical figure.
  • क्षेत्रफल (kṣetraphala): Area.
  • क्षेप (kṣepa) (= नति): Celestial latitude; additive quantity.
  • क्षेप्य (kṣepya): Additive quantity.
  • ग्रह (graha): Planet, heavenly body.
  • खगोल (khagola): Sphere of the sky.
  • खण्डग्रहण (khaṇḍagrahaṇa): Partial eclipse.
  • खण्डज्या (khaṇḍajyā): Sine segment.
  • खमध्य (khamadhya): Zenith.
  • खेचर (khecara): Planet, heavenly body.
  • गणित (gaṇita): Mathematics.
  • गत (gata): Elapsed number or portion of days, nāḍīs, prāṇas, etc.
  • गतिकला (gatikālā): Motion in terms of minutes.
  • गतिविशेष (gativiśeṣa): Difference in motion.
  • गुण (guṇa) (= ज्या): Rsine.
  • गुणकार (guṇakāra): Multiplier.
  • गुणन (guṇana): Multiplication.
  • गुण्य (guṇya): Multiplicand.
  • गुर्वक्षर (gurvakṣara): Unit of time equal to 2/5 of a second (or 4 prāṇas).
  • गोल (gola): Sphere; celestial sphere.
  • ग्रहगति (grahagati): Motion or daily motion of a planet.
  • ग्रहण (grahaṇa): Eclipse.
  • ग्रहयुद्ध (grahayuddha): Planetary war, conjunction of planets with specific characteristics.
  • ग्रहयोग (grahayoga): Conjunction of two planets.
  • ग्रास (grāsa): Obscuration; measure of eclipse.
  • ग्राहक (grāhaka): Eclipsing body.
  • ग्राह्य (grāhya): Eclipsed body.
  • घटिका (ghaṭikā): Unit of time equal to 24 minutes.
  • घटिकामण्डल (ghaṭikāmaṇḍala): Celestial equator.
  • घन (ghana): Cube.
  • घनमध्य (ghanamadhya): Centre of a sphere.
  • चक्रकला (cakrakalā): Minutes contained in a circle (21600).
  • चक्रांश (cakrāṃśa): Degrees contained in a circle (360).
  • चतुर्युग (caturyuga): The four aeons combined (4,320,000 years).
  • चन्द्रग्रहण (candragrahaṇa): Lunar eclipse.
  • चन्द्रोच्च (candrocca): Higher apsis (apogee) of the Moon.
  • चर (cara): Declinational ascensional difference.
  • चरज्या (carajyā): Rsine of caradala.
  • चरदल (caradala) (= चरार्ध): Half ascensional difference.
  • चरप्राण (caraprāṇa): Prāṇas of ascensional difference.
  • चान्द्र (cāndra): Lunar.
  • चाप (cāpa): Arc.
  • चार (cāra): Motion.
  • छादक (chādaka) (= ग्राहक): Eclipsing body.
  • छाद्य (chādya) (= ग्राह्य): Eclipsed body.
  • छाया (chāyā): Shadow; Rsine of zenith distance.
  • छेद (cheda): Denominator.
  • जीवा (jīvā) (= ज्या): Rsine.
  • जूक (jūka): Sign Libra (Tulā).
  • ज्या (jyā): Rsine.
  • ज्याखण्ड (jyākhaṇḍa): Sine segment; Rsine difference.
  • ज्योतिश्चक्र (jyotiścakra): Circle of asterisms.
  • तन्त्र (tantra): Astronomical manual starting from the epoch of a yuga.
  • तमस् (tamas): Node of the Moon (especially ascending node).
  • ताराग्रह (tārāgraha): Star planets (Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn).
  • तिथि (tithi): Lunar day.
  • तुङ्ग (tuṅga) (= उच्च): Higher apsis (apogee).
  • तुला (tulā): Sign Libra.
  • त्रिज्या (trijyā): Radius of the standard circle (3438’).
  • त्रिभुज (tribhuja): Triangle.
  • त्रैराशिक (trairāśika): Rule of Three.
  • दक्षिणायन (dakṣiṇāyana): Southward motion of the Sun.
  • दक्षिणोत्तरमण्डल (dakṣiṇottaramaṇḍala): Meridian circle.
  • दक्षिणोत्तररेखा (dakṣiṇottararekhā): Meridian line.
  • दल (dala): Half.
  • दिनभुक्ति (dinabhukti): Daily motion.
  • दिव्यवर्ष (divyavarṣa): Divine year (equal to 360 human years).
  • दृक्कर्म (dṛkkarma): Reduction to observation.
  • दृक्क्षेप (dṛkkṣepa): Zenith distance of the nonagesimal or its Rsine.
  • दृक्क्षेपज्या (dṛkkṣepajyā): Rsine drkkṣepa.
  • दृक्क्षेपमण्डल (dṛkkṣepamaṇḍala): Vertical circle through the nonagesimal.
  • दृक्क्षेपलग्न (dṛkkṣepalagna): Nonagesimal.
  • दृग्गति (dṛggati): Rsine of the altitude of the nonagesimal.
  • दृग्गोल (dṛggola): Visible celestial sphere.
  • दृग्ज्या (dṛgjyā): Rsine of the zenith distance.
  • दृग्वृत्त (dṛgvṛttā): Vertical circle.
  • देशान्तर (deśāntara): Terrestrial longitude; correction for terrestrial longitude.
  • देशान्तरकाल (deśāntarakāla): Time difference due to terrestrial longitude.
  • देशान्तरसंस्कार (deśāntarasaṃskāra): Correction for local longitude.
  • दोर्ज्या (dorjyā): Rsine of the bhuja.
  • द्युगण (dyugaṇa): Days elapsed since epoch.
  • द्युगत (dyugata): Time elapsed from sunrise.
  • द्युज्या (dyujyā): Day radius.
  • द्युदल (dyudala): Midday.
  • द्युवृत्त (dyuvṛttā) (= अहोरात्रवृत्त): Diurnal circle.
  • धन (dhana): Positive; additive.
  • धनुः (dhanuḥ): Arc.
  • ध्रुव (dhruva): Celestial pole; zero position of a planet at epoch.
  • ध्रुवोन्नति (dhruvonnati): Elevation of the celestial pole.
  • नक्षत्र (nakṣatra): Star, asterism.
  • नक्षत्रदिन (nakṣatradina): Sidereal day.
  • नत (nata): Zenith distance.
  • नतज्या (natajyā): Rsine of zenith distance.
  • नतनाडी (natanāḍī): Interval in nāḍīs from midday.
  • नतांश (natāṃśa): Degrees of zenith distance.
  • नतशिञ्जिनी (nataśiñjinī) (= नतज्या): Rsine zenith distance.
  • नति (nati): Parallax in latitude.
  • नाडिका (nāḍikā) (= नाडी, घटिका): Unit of time (24 minutes).
  • निपात (nipāta): Immersion (in eclipse).
  • निरक्ष (nirakṣa): Terrestrial equator.
  • निरक्षक्षितिज (nirakṣakṣitija): Equatorial horizon.
  • नीच (nīca): Perigee or perihelion.
  • नीचोच्चवृत्त (nīcovṛtta): Epicycle.
  • पक्ष (pakṣa): Half of a lunar month.
  • पद (pada): Quadrant of a circle.
  • पदच्छाया (padacchāyā) (= पदमा): Gnomonic shadow measured in feet.
  • परमग्रास (paramagrāsa): Maximum obscuration in an eclipse.
  • परमापक्रम (paramāpakrama): Maximum declination (24°).
  • परिधि (paridhi): Circumference.
  • परिलेख (parilekha): Diagram, graphical representation.
  • परीक्षा (parīkṣā): Experimentation, observation.
  • पर्यय (paryaya) (= भगण): Revolutions of a planet in a yuga.
  • पर्व (parva) (= पर्वान्त): End point of the new/full moon.
  • पलज्या (palajyā): Rsine latitude.
  • पलभा (palabhā): Equinoctial shadow.
  • पात (pāta): Node (especially ascending node).
  • पूर्वापर (pūrvāpara): Prime vertical; east-west line.
  • पृष्ठ (pṛṣṭha): Surface.
  • प्रग्रह (pragraha): First contact in an eclipse.
  • प्रतिमण्डल (pratimaṇḍala): Eccentric circle.
  • प्रमाण (pramāṇa): Argument in the Rule of Three.
  • प्राग्लग्न (prāglagna): Ascendant.
  • प्राण (prāṇa): Unit of time equal to 4 sidereal seconds.
  • प्राणकलान्तर (prāṇakalāntara): Difference in minutes expressed in prāṇas.
  • फल (phala): Fruit in the Rule of Three; equation.
  • बाहु (bāhu) (= भुज): Lateral side of a right-angled triangle.
  • बिम्ब (bimba): Disc of a celestial body.
  • बीज (bīja): Astronomical correction applied to mean planets.
  • भगण (bhagaṇa): Revolutions of a planet in a yuga.
  • भगोल (bhagola): Sphere of asterisms, celestial sphere.
  • भचक्र (bhacakra): Ecliptic circle.
  • भाग (bhāga) (= अंश): Degree.
  • भुक्ति (bhukti): Daily motion.
  • भुज (bhuja) (= भुजा): Side of a right-angled triangle; longitude in a quadrant.
  • भुजज्या (bhujajyā) (= दोर्ज्या): Rsine of bhuja.
  • भुजान्तरफल (bhujāntaraphala): Correction for equation of time due to eccentricity.
  • भुजाफल (bhujāphala): Equation of the centre.
  • भू (bhū) (= भूमि): Earth.
  • भूगोल (bhūgola): Sphere of the Earth.
  • भूच्छाया (bhūcchāyā): Earth’s shadow.
  • भूदिन (bhūdina) (= सावनदिन): Terrestrial civil day.
  • भूभ्रम (bhūbhrama): Rotation of the Earth.
  • भूवृत्त (bhūvṛttā): Circumference of the Earth.
  • भोग (bhoga): Motion; position.
  • मकर (makara) (= मृग): Sign Capricorn.
  • मण्डल (maṇḍala): Circle; orb.
  • मध्यगति (madhyagati): Mean motion.
  • मध्यग्रहण (madhyagrahaṇa): Middle of eclipse.
  • मध्यच्छाया (madhyacchāyā): Midday shadow.
  • मध्यम (madhyama): Mean planet / position.
  • मन्द (manda): Slow; apogee (mandocca).
  • मन्दकर्म (mandakarma): Manda operation in planetary computation.
  • मन्दकेन्द्र (mandakendra): Manda anomaly.
  • मन्दफल (mandaphala): Equation of the centre.
  • मन्दपरिधि (mandaparidhi): Epicycle of the equation of the centre.
  • मन्दोच्च (mandocca): Apogee or aphelion.
  • महाच्छाया (mahācchāyā): Rsine of zenith distance.
  • महाशङ्कु (mahāśaṅku): Rsine of altitude.
  • मुहूर्त (muhūrta): Unit of time equal to 48 minutes.
  • मूल (mūla) (= वर्गमूल): Square root.
  • मेषादि (meṣādi): First point of Aries.
  • मोक्ष (mokṣa) (= विमोक्ष): Last contact / emersion in an eclipse.
  • मौढ्य (mauḍhya): Combustion of a planet.
  • याम्य (yāmya): Southern.
  • याम्योत्तररेखा (yāmyottararekhā): Meridian line.
  • युग (yuga): Aeon.
  • युग्म (yugma): Even quadrant.
  • योग (yoga): Conjunction of planets.
  • योजन (yojana): Unit of distance (approx. 7.5 miles).
  • योजनव्यास (yojanavyāsa): Diameter in yojanas.
  • राशि (rāśi): Sign of the zodiac (30°).
  • राशिकूट (rāśikūṭa): Nonagesimal point of the ecliptic.
  • राशिगोल (rāśigola) (= भगोल): Celestial sphere.
  • राहु (rāhu) (= पात): Ascending node of the Moon.
  • लग्न (lagna): Rising point of the ecliptic.
  • लङ्कोदय (laṅkōdaya): Right ascension of signs.
  • लम्ब (lamba): Co-latitude; Rsine of co-latitude.
  • लम्बन (lambana): Parallax in longitude.
  • लिप्ता (liptā) (= कला): Minute of arc.
  • लिप्ताव्यास (liptāvyāsa): Angular diameter in minutes.
  • वक्र (vakra): Retrograde motion.
  • वर्ग (varga): Square.
  • वर्गमूल (vargamūla): Square root.
  • विलोम (viloma): Retrograde.
  • विक्षेप (vikṣepa): Celestial latitude.
  • विमण्डल (vimaṇḍala) (= विक्षेपमण्डल): Orbit of a planet.
  • विनाडिका (vināḍikā) (= चषक): Unit of time equal to 24 seconds (1/60 of a nāḍī).
  • विमर्द (vimarda): Duration of total eclipse.
  • विवर (vivara): Difference.
  • विशेष (viśeṣa): Difference.
  • विषम (viṣama): Odd quadrant.
  • विषुवत् (viṣuvat): Equinox.
  • विषुवच्छाया (viṣuvacchāyā): Equinoctial midday shadow.
  • वृत्त (vṛttā): Circle.
  • व्यास (vyāsa): Diameter.
  • व्यासार्ध (vyāsārdha): Radius.
  • शङ्कु (śaṅku): Gnomon; Rsine of altitude.
  • शङ्कुकोटि (śaṅkukoṭi): Rsine of zenith distance.
  • शर (śara): Arrow; Rversed sine.
  • शिष्ट (śiṣṭa) (= शेष): Remainder.
  • शीघ्र (śīghra): Fast; śīghrocca.
  • शीघ्रकर्ण (śīghrakarṇa): Geocentric vector.
  • शीघ्रकर्म (śīghrakarma): Planetary equation of conjunction.
  • शीघ्रोच्च (śīghrocca): Apex of conjunction.
  • शून्य (śūnya): Zero.
  • शोध्य (śodhya): Subtractive quantity.
  • संवर्ग (saṃvarga) (= गुणन): Multiplication.
  • संक्रान्ति (saṃkrānti): Transit of the Sun into a zodiacal sign.
  • सममण्डल (samamaṇḍala): Prime vertical.
  • सममण्डलच्छाया (samamaṇḍalacchāyā): Shadow when the Sun is on the prime vertical.
  • समस्तग्रहण (samastagrahaṇa): Total eclipse.
  • सम्पर्कार्ध (samparkārdha): Semi-sum of the diameters of the eclipsed and eclipsing bodies.
  • सायन (sāyana): With precession added.
  • सावनदिन (sāvanadina): Civil day (sunrise to sunrise).
  • सूर्यग्रहण (sūryagrahaṇa): Solar eclipse.
  • सौम्य (saumya): Northern.
  • सौर (saura): Solar.
  • स्थित्यर्ध (sthityardha): Half-duration of an eclipse.
  • स्फुट (sphuṭa): True position / value.
  • स्फुटगति (sphuṭagati): True daily motion.
  • स्फुटनति (sphuṭanati): True parallax in latitude.
  • स्फुटविक्षेप (sphuṭavikṣepa): True celestial latitude corrected for parallax.
  • क्षेप (kṣepa): Additive quantity.
  • स्वदेशराशिमान (svadeśarāśimāna): Local oblique ascension of a sign.

item.title: ६ SUBJECT INDEX
item.description:

    • Justification of Lalla’s epoch for the Bhaṭa-correction, 19–20

    • Com. Laghuvivṛti, xxxiii, xxxiv;

item.title: ७ BIBLIOGRAPHY
item.description:
APPENDIX V: BIBLIOGRAPHY

  1. Āryabhaṭīya of Āryabhaṭa (b. A.D. 476):
    • Cr. ed. with Intro., Eng. Tr., Notes, Comments and four Indexes, by K. S. Shukla and K. V. Sarma, Indian National Sc. Academy, New Delhi, 1976.
    • Cr. ed. with the commentary of Bhāskara I, with Intro. and nine Appendices, by K. S. Shukla, Indian National Sc. Academy, New Delhi, 1976.
    • Cr. ed. with the commentary of Sūryadeva-yajvan, with Intro. and three Appendices, by K. V. Sarma, Indian National Sc. Academy, New Delhi, 1976.
    • Pt. I. Gaṇitapāda and Pt. II. Kālakriyāpāda, ed. with the com. entitled Mahābhāṣya of Nīlakaṇṭha Somasutvan, by K. Sambasiva Śāstrī, Trivandrum, 1930, 1931; Pt. III. Golapāda, ed. by Śūranāḍ Kuñjan Piḷḷai, 1957, (TSS, 101, 110, 185).
    • Tr. into Eng. by P. C. Sengupta, J of the Deptt. of Letters, Calcutta Univ., 16 (1927) 1–56.
  2. Bhāgavat-Purāṇa:
    • Pub. Gita Press, Gorakhpur, Sam. 1999.
  3. Brahmasphuṭasiddhānta of Brahmagupta (A.D. 628):
    • Ed. by Sudhākara Dvivedī with his own com., Benares, 1902.
    • Ed. with Sudhākara’s com. and his own com. Vāsanā and Hindi Tr. by Ram Swarup Sharma, with Introduction by Satya Prakash, 4 vols., New Delhi, 1966.
  4. Bṛhatsaṃhitā of Varāhamihira (d. A.D. 587):
    • Ed. with the com. of Bhaṭṭotpala, by Sudhākara Dvivedī, 2 vols., Benares, 1895–97, (Vizianagaram Skt. Ser., No. 10); by Avadh Bihari Tripathi, 1968, (Sarasvati Bhavana Granthamālā, No. 97).
    • (Tr. in Eng): Complete system of Natural Astrology, by H. Kern, Verspreide Geschriften (Collected Works), ’s-Gravenhage, 1913.
    • Ed. with Eng. Tr. by V. Subrahmanya Sastri and M. Ramakrishna Bhat, 2 vols., Bangalore, 1947.
  5. Candracchāyāgaṇita of Nīlakaṇṭha Somayāji:
    • Cr. ed. with Auto-commentary, Introduction, Translation and two Appendices, by K. V. Sarma, Hoshiarpur, 1976.
  6. Dṛggaṇita of Parameśvara (A.D. 1360–1455):
    • Cr. ed. with Intro., and Appendix by K. V. Sarma, V.V.R. Inst., Hoshiarpur, 1963.
  7. Goladīpikā (1) of Parameśvara (A.D. 1360–1455):
    • Ed. by T. Ganapati Sastri, Trivandrum, 1916, (TSS, No. 49).
  8. Goladīpikā (2) of Parameśvara (A.D. 1360–1455):
    • Cr. ed. with auto-com., Intro. and Tr., by K. V. Sarma, Adyar, 1957.
  9. Golasāra of Nīlakaṇṭha Somayāji (A.D. 1444–1545):
    • Cr. ed. with Intro. and Tr., by K. V. Sarma, Hoshiarpur, 1970.
  10. Grahacāranibandhana of Haridatta (c. A.D. 650–700):
    • Cr. ed. with Intro. and App., Grahacāranibandhana-saṃgraha, by K. V. Sarma, K.S.R. Inst., Madras, 1954.
  11. Kālanirṇaya (Kālamādhava) of Sāyaṇa-Mādhava:
    • Ed. by Ratna Gopal Bhatta, Benares, 1909.
  12. Karaṇaratna of Deva (A.D. 689):
    • Cr. ed. with Intro., Tr. and Notes etc., by K. S. Shukla. Ms.
  13. Kāśikā-vṛtti with Padamañjarī of Haradatta:
    • Ed. by Dwarikadasa Shastri and Kalikaprasada Shukla, Prachya Bharati Prakasan, Varanasi, 6 vols., 1965 ff.
  14. Khaṇḍakhādyaka of Brahmagupta (A.D. 665):
    • Ed. with Vāsanābhāṣya of Āmarāja, ed. by Babuaji Misra, Calcutta Univ., 1925.
    • Ed. with the com. of Caturveda Pṛthūdakasvāmin (A.D. 864), ed. by P. C. Sengupta, Calcutta Univ., 1941.
    • Cr. ed. with the com. of Bhaṭṭotpala and Tr. in Eng., by Bina Chatterji, 2 vols., World Press, Calcutta, 1970.
  15. Laghu-Bhāskarīya of Bhāskara I (A.D. 629):
    • Cr. ed with the com. Vivaraṇa of Śaṅkaraṇārāyaṇa, Trivandrum, 1949, (TSS, No. 162).
    • Ed. with the com. of Parameśvara, Poona, (An.SS, No. 128).
    • Cr. ed. with Tr. and Intro., by K. S. Shukla, Lucknow Univ., 1963.
  16. Laghumānasā of Muñjāla (A.D. 932):
    • Ed. with com. of Parameśvara, Poona, 1944, (An. SS, No. 123).
    • Ed. with Tr. and critical notes, by N. K. Majumdar; Calcutta, 1951.
    • Cr. edn, with com. Vāsanābhāṣya of Sūryadeva-yajvan, by K. V. Sarma, Ms.
  17. Līlāvatī of Bhāskara II (b. A.D. 1114):
    • Cr. ed. with Kriyākramakarī, an elaborate exposition of the rationale, by Śaṅkara (A.D. 1534) and Nārāyaṇa (A.D. 1607), with Intro. and five Appendices by K. V. Sarma, Hoshiarpur, 1975.
    • Ed. with Colebrooke’s Tr. with Notes, by Haran Chandra Banerji, 2nd edn., Calcutta, 1927.
  18. Mahā-Bhāskarīya of Bhāskara I (A.D. 629):
    • Cr. ed. with the Bhāṣya of Govindasvāmin and the supercommentary Siddhāntadīpikā by Parameśvara, with Intro. and Appendices, by T. S. Kuppanna Sastri, Madras, 1957, (Madras Govt. Or. Ser., No. 130).
    • Ed. with the com. Karmadīpikā by Parameśvara, Poona, 1945, (An.SS, No. 126).
    • Cr. ed. and Tr. into., with Intro., by K. S. Shukla, Lucknow, 1960.
  19. Mahābhāṣya of Patañjali with Pradīpa of Kaiyaṭa:
    • Ed. by N. S. Press, Bombay, 1908.
  20. Pañcasiddhāntikā of Varāhamihira (d. A.D. 587):
    • Ed. with an original com. in Skt. by Sudhākara Dvivedī and Eng. Tr. by G. Thibaut, Benares, 1889; Rep., Motilal Banarsidas, Benares, 1930; Rep., Varanasi, 1968, (Chaukhamba Skt. Studies, No 68).
    • Pt. I. Text and Tr., by D. Pingree; Pt. II. Com. (in Eng.), by O. Neugebauer and D. Pingree, Royal Danish Academy of Sciences and Letters, Copenhagen, 1970–71.
  21. Siddhāntadarpaṇa of Nīlakaṇṭha Somayāji (A.D. 1444–1545):
    • Cr. ed with Tr. and App., by K. V. Sarma, Hoshiarpur, 1976.
  22. Siddhāntasekhara of Śrīpati (A.D. 1039):
    • Ed. with com. of Makkibhaṭṭa (for chs. 1–4) and com. of Babuaji Misra (for chs 5–20), 2 Pts., Calcutta Univ., 1932, 1947.
  23. Śiṣyadhīvṛddhida of Lalla (or Ralla) (A.D. 8th cent.):
    • Ed. by Sudhākara Dvivedi, Benares, 1886.
    • With the com. of Mallikārjuna Sūri (A.D. 1178), Cr. ed. by Bina Chatterji, National Sc. Academy, New Delhi, 1977.
    • With the com. of Bhāskara II (b. A.D. 1114). Ms.
  24. Sūrya-siddhānta (bet. A.D. 650–950):
    • Ed. with the com. Vivaraṇa of Parameśvara, and Cr. Intro., by K. S. Shukla, Lucknow Univ., 1957.
    • Tr. with Notes by E. Burgess and W. D. Whitney, J Am. Or. Soc., 6 (1859–60) 141–498; Sep. rep. of above, Johnson Reprint, New York, 1964; Rep. ed. with Intro., by P. C. Sengupta, Calcutta Univ., 1935.
  25. Taittirīya-Āraṇyaka:
    • Ed. with Bhāṣya of Sāyaṇa-Mādhava, Anandasrama Press, Poona, 1898, (An.SS, No. 36).
  26. Tantrasaṅgraha of Nīlakaṇṭha Somayāji (A.D. 1444–1545):
    • Ed. with the com. Laghuvivṛti of Śaṅkara Variyar, Trivandrum, 1958, (TSS, No. 188).
    • Text with Yuktidīpikā and Laghuvivṛti of Śaṅkara (An elaborate exposition of the rationale of Hindu astronomy). Cr. ed. with Intro. and four Appendices, by K. V. Sarma, Hoshiarpur, 1977.
  27. Tantravārttika of Kumārila Bhaṭṭa:
    • Ed. with com. Nyāyasudhā of Someśvara, Benares, 1901–9, (Ch.SS, No. 14).
  28. Vākyakaraṇa, ascribed to Vararuci (c. A.D. 1300):
    • Cr. ed. with com. Laghuprakāśikā of Sundararāja, with Intro. and App., by T. S. Kuppanna Sastri and K. V. Sarma, K.S.R. Inst., Madras-4, 1962.
  29. Yuktibhāṣā (Mal.) of Jyeṣṭhadeva:
    • Ed. with Notes by Rama Varma Maru Thampuran and A. R. Akhileśvara Iyer, Mangalodayam, Trichur, Pt. I, 1949.
    • Ed. with Notes by K. V. Sarma, Pt. II. Ms.

item.title: ८ ADDENDA ET CORRIGENDA
item.description:
Page Line For Read
x 19 दर्शितम दर्शितम्
xiv 5 ( pp. 36 ) ( pp. 3, 6 )
xxvii 3 1931 1939
3 — After इति । -4 Add: ( कैयटः, महाभाष्यप्रदीपः on पाणिनि, २.४.२६, vār. ७, edn. N. S. Press, 1912, vol. II, p. 545. “तेनादृष्टं च भाष्यकृत्” इति । Add note: The passage is not found quoted in the available Prakīrṇakāṇḍa of Bhartṛhari’s Vākyapadīya. However, the whole verse is found quoted by Haradatta in the Introduction to his com. Padamañjarī on the Kāśikāvṛtti (edn.: Prachya Bharati Prakashan, Varanasi, vol. I, 1965, p. 9:
“तथा च यद् विस्मृतमदृष्टं वा सूत्रकारेण तत् स्फुटम् ।
वाक्यकारी ब्रवीत्येवं तेनादृष्टं च भाष्यकृत् ॥”
8 10 कालयुक्त्यो कालमुक्त्यो
12 5 शके शाके
15 6 अत अतः
16 -10 सर्वम् न्यायेवेयम् सर्वं न्याय्यं
16 — — Add ref.: (Bhāgavata Purāṇa, ११.२२.२५)
23 6 २३६ २४६
26 4 ऋणस्य परिधी ऋणस्वपरिधी
26 17 ऋणस्य संज्ञं ऋणस्वसंज्ञं
35 12-13 — Add fn to अस्माभिः [नीलकण्ठेन] दृष्टानि — Nīlakaṇṭha often gives the data about eclipses seen by him. Cf. his Bhāṣya on Ā.Bh., Kālakriyā, 12-15, edn. TSS 111, p. 23: “दृष्टश्चास्माभिः समस्तग्रासः सूर्यस्य ‘हंसो विहतं ताप’ इति (Kali day 16,68,478 in A.D. 1522). ‘ह्रासवृद्ध्यादितो ऽर्कः’ इति द्युगणे (Kali day 16,81,472 in A.D. 1537) मध्यतमस्कं चाभूदनन्तक्षेत्रे । तत्र आर्धरात्रिकोक्तानां स्थौल्यं चावगतम्।”
Incidentally, this latest discovery would mean that the date of Ā.Bh. Bhāṣya has to be pushed down, at least to A.D. 1537 and N’s last date still further down.
32 — — Delete line 10-11
41 2 २२८८ २२६६’ ४०"
42 5 २२६७’ ४०" —

44 2 — Delete footnote 4
